author: JungyupLee
name: PR #1855 stack + MP3 marker-pair fusion + alias smear boundary
blurb: Built on PR #1855 (11L XSA + LQER asym int4 + SparseAttnGate + BOS-fixed SmearGate + Polar-Express MuonEqR + fused softcapped CE + GPTQ int6 + int7 embed + per-row int8 attn-gate + per-group lrzip+brotli + phased TTT + 9-hparam stack). Two orthogonal additions: (1) MP3 marker-pair fusion — 3 alias donor tokens for [SPACE,TITLE]/[SPACE,ALLCAPS]/[SPACE,CAPNEXT], 8.47% train-token saving, word X preserved (no d=1 collapse); (2) alias smear boundary — SmearGate fully disabled (ALIAS_PREV_SMEAR_SCALE=0.0) at positions immediately following an alias token, since alias donors already encode dense (SPACE + marker) signal at one position. Code patch: +62/-2 lines vs PR #1855's train_gpt.py. 3-seed mean (42, 0, 314) phased TTT val_bpb 1.05858203 beats PR #1855 author's 3-seed mean 1.06108 by 0.00250 BPB.
eval: phased TTT (3 cumulative phases at doc-boundaries 833/1666/2500, LoRA rank=80)
technique_summary: PR #1855 stack + MP3 marker-pair fusion (3 alias donors, 8.47% token saving) + alias smear boundary (ALIAS_PREV_SMEAR_SCALE=0.0)
note: After deadline, the canonical 50K-val-doc partition was rebuilt single-pass on a local DGX (val_tokens 43,767,808). The originally submitted runpod runs had val_tokens 36,562,944 due to a 4-way parallel data-prep step that did not produce the strict global first-50K val partition. The numbers in this file reflect the DGX corrected canonical reproduction with byte-identical train_gpt.py (md5 f4a81ac75c987126f330ee30f888ee57), identical seeds, and identical hyperparameters.
